title: physaria kingii subsp. cobrensis
intro:
Physaria kingii subsp. cobrensis, commonly known as King's Curse or Cobrensis Physaria, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the southwestern United States. As a medicinal plant, it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The therapeutic actions of Physaria kingii subsp. cobrensis include decongesting the respiratory system, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential uses.

Note: The information provided is based on available data and might be limited due to the rarity of this plant species. Further research is required for a comprehensive understanding of its medicinal properties.

parts: Physaria kingii subsp. cobrensis has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, which contain isorhapontigenin and its glycosides, compounds with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and stems of this plant contain physalin and its derivatives, which may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The flowers of Physaria kingii subsp. cobrensis contain carotenoid pigments, including β-carotene and lycopene, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. side_effects: Physaria kingii subsp. cobrensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of saponins.
